description_long: This object is a set of three modern traffic lights, with one mounted on a tall, L-shaped overhead arm and two on shorter vertical poles. Each signal head features three circular lenses arranged vertically, displaying the standard red, yellow, and green colors. The housing of the lights is rectangular with rounded edges, and the poles are sleek and metallic, likely made of steel or aluminum. The overhead arm extends horizontally to position the main signal above a roadway, while the two side signals are positioned for visibility at street level. The lights are encased in a smooth, light-colored frame, and the lenses are clearly visible from the front. No pedestrian signals or additional signage are present, and the design is utilitarian and streamlined for urban environments.
description: A set of three vertically-aligned traffic lights, one on an overhead arm and two on upright poles, each with red, yellow, and green lenses in a modern, metallic housing.
description_view_0: The traffic lights are seen from an angle showing the overhead arm and the three signal heads, with the main light elevated above the others.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the thinness of the poles and the signal heads, with the colored lenses barely visible.
description_view_2: A frontal view displays all three traffic lights clearly, with the red, yellow, and green lenses in vertical alignment.
description_view_3: Another side profile, similar to view 1, showing the slim structure of the lights and poles.
